spec_match.rationale: The page registers real Highlight objects, shows highlight-chain inheritance, and now includes an overlapping spell/grammar range whose painting order is controlled through Highlight.priority.

interactive.rationale: Four registry toggles, clear-all, and priority sliders update CSS.highlights, button states, priority values, and the live winner readout.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The concept and feature index both describe layered Highlight registries and highlight inheritance, and the demo now also exposes the priority mechanism for overlapping ranges.

edge_cases.rationale: The page handles missing Custom Highlight support, detects Highlight.priority support, and demonstrates overlapping ranges rather than only independent non-conflicting highlights.

references.rationale: The see-also section now includes the mandatory ChromeStatus entry plus the CSS Custom Highlight API spec.

design_system.rationale: The active tag buttons use shared CSS variables, no literal white, and choose dark text on amber/emerald accents for small-text contrast.

summary: Resolved the custom-highlight-api critique by adding the ChromeStatus reference, replacing literal white contrast pairs, adding overlapping spell/grammar ranges controlled by Highlight.priority sliders, and documenting the character-offset Range pattern in the code listing.
